_notes: Priority queue for the SBU AI cluster (defq): h100 = 4x H100 80GB; quadro1-2 = 8x RTX 8000 48GB; tesla1-2 = 8x V100 32GB; orion = 4x V100 + 1.5TB RAM.

Queue-state reality check (2026-07-08): h100 and orion are fully allocated by multi-day jobs; free GPUs exist on quadro1/tesla1 and trickle elsewhere. So: short time limits + 1 GPU = good backfill odds; nothing is pinned to h100 before the QF deadline.

Priorities 1-9 are single-GPU models that fit even a V100 32GB (or at least an RTX 8000) - they go first. 10-12 need multiple 48GB GPUs (exclude keeps them off 32GB V100 nodes) and run after the safe tier.
est_weights_gb is the approximate q4 download/VRAM footprint of the default Ollama quantization; add ~15-25% for KV-cache/overhead when choosing GPUs.
tag_checked=false means the tag was read off ollama.com/library on 2026-07-08 but not yet pulled here - verify via a prefetch job (a wrong tag fails immediately, nothing is wasted).
gres/nodelist/exclude/mem/time override queue_defaults when run_queue.sh submits. mem is deliberately generous because Ollama mmaps weights and Slurm's memory cgroup counts that page cache.
Default is one job after another (--dependency chain); use run_queue.sh --parallel when racing a kickoff.

models.why: Fast, reasoning-capable, fits any GPU on the cluster. Ideal first real run to shake down the pipeline.
Newest Google open model (library lists 12b/26b/31b). 26b is the quality/speed sweet spot; 31b also fits a single RTX 8000.
Reasoning model - does chain-of-thought help score prediction? Full R1/V4 don't fit the cluster; this distill does. Longer time limit: thinking tokens are slow.
GLM representative that runs locally (30B class). GLM-5.x only ships as a cloud tag - see not_feasible.
Mistral representative (24B). Mistral Large 3 is a big MoE; check its size on the library before attempting.
Strong recent open family. ~22GB weights + KV is too tight for a 32GB V100, so V100 nodes are excluded.
Strong small model; runs anywhere including V100 nodes.
Small baseline - anchors the leaderboard's low end and answers 'does scale matter here?'
Tiny baseline; nearly free to run.
Meta's 70B flagship (3.3 supersedes 3.1-70b at the same size). 2x RTX 8000 (96GB); h100 is occupied for days, but 1x H100 also works when free.
The big gpt-oss - direct 20b vs 120b scaling comparison. MoE with ~5B active params, so it's fast once loaded. 2x RTX 8000 or 1x H100.

not_feasible.reason: Cloud-only: the library's single tag is minimax-m2.7:cloud (229B). Confirmed empirically - job 45428 failed with 'pull model manifest: file does not exist' for the bare tag on 2026-07-11. No downloadable weights exist.

glm-5.2 IS on the library (756B, 1M context) but its only tag is glm-5.2:cloud - an Ollama-cloud-hosted model with no downloadable weights, so it cannot run on cluster GPUs. Running it would also mean sending data to Ollama's cloud service, outside this benchmark's local-inference scope. Locally, 7xxB MoE exceeds any node's total VRAM (max 384GB on quadro nodes).
Frontier-size MoE, same VRAM problem as GLM-5.x.
~230GB q4 dense (not MoE) - would be brutally slow even if sharded across 8x RTX 8000.
